rulings.Case Title: RIMMA BOROKHOV et al VS. FREEDOM WEST HOMES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Defendant Freedom West Homes, Inc To Produce Resident Files And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 26, 2014, line 1. PLAINTIFFs RIMMA BOROKHOV, DAVID TSE and MICHAEL TO's Motion To Compel Defendant Freedom West Homes, Inc To Produce Resident Files And For Monetary Sanctions Continued to July 7, 2014, on the Court's Own Motion. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ZELJKO MARKOVIC VS. COUNTRYWIDE et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 26, 2014, line 2. DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'s DEMURRER to 1st Amended Complaint Defendant's demurrer to the Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint is SUSTAINED. The demurrer is first cause of action is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND as to the claim of violation of Cal. Civil Code section 2923.5. Defendant did not violate Cal. Civil Code section 2923.5. The demurrer is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND as to the breach of Breach of Implied Covenant of Good faith and fair dealing. Plaintiff failed to state any specific actions or conduct that may have resulted in a breach of the terms of the deed of trust. The demurrer is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND as to the Second cause of action for Fraud. Plaintiff failed to state specific and particular instances of the fraud. The demurrer is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND as to the Third cause of action for Intentional Misrepresentation and Fifth cause of action for Violation of Cal. Civil Code section 1572. Plaintiff failed to allege sufficient facts. The demurrer is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND as to the Fourth Cause of Action for Quiet Title. Plaintiff failed to allege the ability to tender the amount of unpaid debt. Finally, as to the Declaratory Relief Cause of Action, the demurrer is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. This cause of action cannot stand independently of the others, as to which the Court has sustained this demurrer. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID SALMA et al VS. RUBEN DIEGO Q PICARDO et al (Pltfs Challenge Judge Quidachay)
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 26, 2014, line 3. DEFENDANT KATRINA RODRIGUEZ MONTINOLA's DEMURRER to 1st Amended Complaint Defendant's demurrer as to all COAs claimed by Plaintiff Salma against Defendant Montinola is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. Plaintiff Palma has no standing to bring claims against Defendant Montinola. Defendant's demurrer is OVERRULED as to the third cause of action for Quiet title. Plaintiff Zaborski has pled some instances of fraud that justify a cause of action for quiet title by the holder of an equitable title. Defendant's demurrer is OVERRULED as to the fifth cause of action for Unfair Competition. The fraudulent short sale falls within Business and professions code section 17200. Defendant's demurrer is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND as to the Sixth COA for Constructive Trust. While Plaintiff alleges some instances of fraud, Plaintiff has failed to include Defendant Montonola in the cause of action for fraud. Defendant's demurrer is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND as to the Seventh COA for appointment of a receiver. Plaintiff failed to state sufficient facts regarding an appointment of a receiver. Defendant's demurrer is OVERRULED as to the eighth COA for declaratory relief. Demurrer was based on the argument regarding the inadequately pled Quiet Title Cause of Action; however, the court has overruled the demurrer as to quiet title. Defendant's demurrer as to the ninth cause of action for Unjust Enrichment is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. Unjust enrichment is not a cause of action. See Hill v. Roll Intern. Corp. (2011) 195 Cal.App.4th 1295, 1307; McBride v. Boughton (2004) 123 Cal.App.4th 379, 387. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID SALMA et al VS. RUBEN DIEGO Q PICARDO et al (Pltfs Challenge Judge Quidachay)
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 26, 2014, line 3. DEFENDANT KATRINA RODRIGUEZ MONTINOLA's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Denied. Defendant failed to file separate motions for demurrer and motion to strike. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS C.R. TAM VS. THOMAS W. TAM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 26, 2014, line 5. CROSS DEFENDANT THOMAS TAM's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT DENIED in part and GRANTED in part. The motion is MOOT as to the fifth cause of action for Quiet title since Plaintiff does not seek punitive damages for the fifth cause of action. The motion is GRANTED as to the punitive damages allegation in the Second cause of action for Breach of Contract. Contractual causes of action do not support a basis for punitive damages. The motion is also GRANTED as to the third cause of action for negligent misrepresentation. Punitive damages are not available for negligent misrepresentation. See Alliance Mortgage Co. v. Rothwell (1995) 10 Cal.4th 1226, 1241. Cross-Defendant's Motion to Strike the claim of personal injury damages as to the second cause of action for Breach of Contract is GRANTED. Such damages are not available for a breach of contract. Cross-Defendant's Motion to Strike the claim of emotional distress damages as to the second cause of action for Breach of Contract is DENIED. Cross-Claimant has sufficiently pled a preexisting relationship. Cross-Defendant's Motion to Strike the Court the claim of emotional damages as to the third cause of action for Negligent Misrepresentation is DENIED. Cross-Claimant has sufficiently pled a preexisting relationship. Cross-Defendant's Motion to Strike the claim of personal injury damages as to the third cause of action for Negligent Misrepresentation is GRANTED. Cross0-laimant failed to state sufficient facts. Finally, leave to amend the complaint to allege causes of action for fraud and Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress is GRANTED. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: TED FELDMAN VS. DORIS KOBUCHI et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 26, 2014, line 6. DEFENDANTs DORIS KOBUCHI, ANNA MCCARTHY, SHIRLEY FONG, JEAN FONG and WILBERT FONG's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant's demurrer is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND as to the breach of contract cause of action. Plaintiff must attach a copy of the alleged contract to the complaint. The demurrer is OVERRULED as to the second cause of action for Breach of Implied Covenant of Good Faith and Fair Dealing. Plaintiff has presented sufficient fact. The statute of limitations does not bar this cause of action. The demurrer is OVERRULED as to the third cause of action for fraud. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts regarding the elements of fraud. The demurrer is OVERRULED as to the fourth cause of action for Intentional Interference with Contractual Relationship. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts regarding the breach. The demurrer is OVERRULED as to the fifth cause of action for Negligence. Plaintiff has sufficiently pled facts regarding Defendant's duty. The demurrer is OVERRULED as to the sixth cause of action for specific performance. The terms of this contract are sufficiently certain and clearly ascertainable. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: LUTHER BURBANK SAVINGS, A FEDERALLY CHARTERED VS. WILHELMINA BEINS et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 26, 2014, line 7. Status Report on Preliminary Injunction DENIED. There are "less onerous remedies" available. See Breedlove v. J.W. & E.M. Breedlove Excavating Co., 56 Cal.App.2d 141, 143. Plaintiff's motion for a temporary restraining order is GRANTED. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MANFRED ANGSTENBERGER VS. ROBERT S. WILSON et al
